118100.explanation: We strengthen local democracy by experimenting with a citizens' panel in which residents of Leidschendam-Voorburg are randomly selected. Panel members can provide solicited and unsolicited advice to civil servants, the mayor and aldermen, and the municipal council.

118101.explanation: GL-PvdA believes that residents should be able to actively participate in thinking and acting in managing their environment. Through the right to challenge or similar instruments, they can take over municipal tasks such as managing green spaces or facilities, as long as the quality and interests of the neighborhood are safeguarded.

118102.explanation: GL-PvdA believes that municipal permits should not unnecessarily burden residents. Increases should be limited and carefully considered so that permits remain affordable and accessible, and residents are not financially overburdened.

118106.explanation: We are in solidarity with people fleeing war and violence. That is why we also host refugees in Leidschendam-Voorburg. This helps relieve other municipalities in the Netherlands.

118110.explanation: Fireworks are too burdensome for people and animals, the air quality, and emergency services. That is why we prefer alternatives, such as a drone show.

118111.explanation: We are investigating whether we can introduce diftar (differentiated rates), where households pay less waste tax based on the amount of residual waste they produce.

118112.explanation: GL-PvdA believes noise nuisance at festivals and events should be limited to protect residents and visitors. Sound standards will be clearly established and enforced to minimize inconvenience and health risks.

118115.explanation: The local broadcaster will merge in the long term into a regional structure. In this case, financing will change, including from the municipality. Currently, the local broadcaster naturally receives financial support from the municipality.

118117.explanation: Every child deserves opportunities for broad development. Therefore, we will expand the enriched school day to all primary and secondary schools, with extra activities in sports, culture, nature, and technology, in collaboration with schools and local organizations.

118120.explanation: In our municipality, we stand up for the rights of women, people with a non-Dutch background, and the LGBTQ+ community. We tackle discrimination and street intimidation firmly. Everyone must feel safe and free to move around the streets. Our public order officers play a key role in this.

118121.explanation: GL-PvdA values safety, but camera surveillance should be used sparingly and proportionally. We focus primarily on prevention, neighborhood mediation, and social measures, ensuring privacy and only using cameras where truly necessary.

118122.explanation: The municipality of Leidschendam-Voorburg collaborates on this with the Haaglanden region.

118125.explanation: The municipality of Leidschendam-Voorburg is part of the regional housing market of the Haaglanden region. Therefore, this instrument is less effective.

118128.explanation: Vacant shops, offices, and buildings are converted into affordable housing, primarily for young people, starters, and singles, or for other functions, such as a general practitioner's office.

118129.explanation: We focus on building for every target group. Temporary housing for students is less applicable because Leidschendam-Voorburg has virtually no student population.

118130.explanation: Not everyone receiving social assistance is able to perform socially meaningful work; personal circumstances, health, or caregiving duties can limit this, and we take these into careful and respectful account.

118132.explanation: We raise the income threshold for minimum schemes and the individual income allowance to the social minimum of 150% of the social assistance norm. We monitor whether the schemes are sufficient for our residents to get by. We increase the individual income allowance to €600 per household per year.

118134.explanation: GL-PvdA believes the municipality should always consider residents' needs when facilitating charging points for electric vehicles, so sustainability goes hand in hand with livability, ease of use, and neighborhood support.

118136.explanation: Paid parking helps promote walking, cycling, and public transport use. Parking in residential neighborhoods is only for residents and their visitors.

118138.explanation: Everyone has the right to appropriate care. Care institutions and professionals are able to determine, based on expertise and consultation, which care provider best fits the care needs.

118140.explanation: The municipality must cooperate with schools, community centers, and sports clubs to effectively combat period poverty and ensure no one is limited by a lack of menstrual products.

118144.explanation: All Sundays in the municipality of Leidschendam-Voorburg are shopping Sundays.

118145.explanation: We ensure that recognized refugees can settle here. When people finally receive a residence permit after long procedures, it is important they are quickly offered housing. Leidschendam-Voorburg is in solidarity with other municipalities and contributes to this national task.

118147.explanation: We exclude no forms of sustainable energy. We carefully consider what fits within the municipality and the urbanized area. New technologies like mini district heating networks and very low temperature heating networks offer opportunities, also at the neighborhood level.

118149.explanation: GL-PvdA sees coffee shops as part of a safe and regulated soft drug policy. Sales take place under strict rules to limit nuisance, protect young people, and ensure livability. The municipality determines the distribution and supervision, with attention to quality and safety.

118150.explanation: We build affordable housing. In every new construction project, at least 40% of the homes will be social rental and at least 30% mid-priced rental or owner-occupied. This standard applies to all projects, both in new neighborhoods and existing ones.

118152.explanation: We make better use of existing space by loosening rules for house splitting, adding floors, living with others, and subletting, while maintaining livability. We convert vacant buildings into affordable housing for young people and starters or other functions such as general practitioner offices.

118154.explanation: We maintain and strengthen the compact shopping areas in the centers. The design of core shopping areas is green and thus attractive to visitors. We encourage a diverse range of shops accessible by public transport.

118158.explanation: Vlietland is a vulnerable natural area that we protect. Recreation is only allowed if the natural value is preserved. Recreational housing does not fit here; therefore, we oppose a park with recreational villas by all legal means.

118160.explanation: A new boost is urgently needed for the Julianabaan. A new library and a beautiful, greener square greatly contribute to livability. We will make the Damcentrum car-restricted, creating a pleasant shopping area.

118100.explanation: We stand for a strong local democracy, but referenda are not the right instrument for every important decision. We prefer to focus on better participation, transparent decision-making, and direct consultation moments, so residents have influence without the administration getting stuck.

118101.explanation: We are positive about initiatives where residents themselves manage green spaces or a sports hall, but mainly as a supplement — not as a replacement — to proper municipal maintenance. Municipal maintenance remains a core task.

118102.explanation: The price of a permit should cover the costs of issuing it, but should not become an additional source of revenue for the government.

118106.explanation: We have stipulated a term of 2(+1) years in the permit for the shelter location at Stationsplein. For us, this means no automatic continuation of this shelter location, regardless of whether the Spread Law is still in effect then.

118110.explanation: Fireworks are part of our municipality. We endorse the upcoming national fireworks ban but want a municipal fireworks show. We allow exceptions at neighborhood level for professional fireworks with a permit, for example at weddings.

118111.explanation: We want a simple waste collection system with high service, close to the doorstep, and low costs. We are against Diftar, meaning paying per kilo of waste. Our residents will pay more because of this, while the waste problem is not solved by it.

118112.explanation: Stricter noise standards are unnecessary. Events are part of a lively municipality. We should tackle nuisance with good agreements and enforcement, not with extra rules that only make organizing harder.

118115.explanation: Municipalities have the legal duty to maintain and support the local broadcaster. At the same time, we want it to be neutral and critical where needed. Therefore, we do not want additional support funded by taxpayers.

118117.explanation: Music lessons are valuable, but parents and schools have the primary responsibility. The municipality can then provide targeted help in families where needed.

118120.explanation: We want to increase the number of enforcement officers (boa’s). We also focus on youth boa’s. Enforcement officers will be assigned to a fixed district so residents know exactly whom to contact easily to report unsafe and nuisance situations.

118121.explanation: We want camera surveillance where necessary. Whether it concerns waste hotspots or nuisance locations on the street. Camera surveillance increases the chance of catching offenders and provides monitoring of risk areas.

118122.explanation: Victims of domestic violence must always have a safe place to go, but our municipality does not necessarily need its own women's shelter if there is good regional accommodation. The municipality should mainly ensure rapid assistance and good regional cooperation.

118125.explanation: This creates flow, enabling starters and young families to find housing. We also want priority for residents who work in Leidschendam-Voorburg: police officers, healthcare workers, and teachers.

118128.explanation: Converting vacant offices into housing is a smart way to build faster. But only if it is proven that these offices can no longer be rented.

118129.explanation: Housing shortages should be solved by building and accelerating, not with temporary housing.

118130.explanation: Those receiving social assistance deserve support, but we also ask for a fair reciprocal contribution. Not to punish, but to keep people actively involved in society. A reciprocal contribution helps utilize talents, increases job opportunities, and prevents social isolation.

118132.explanation: We support a safety net for tougher times, but poverty policy must remain focused on those who truly need it. We strongly focus on work or, alternatively, contributing to society according to ability.

118134.explanation: We encourage the use of electric vehicles by ensuring sufficient parking spaces with charging stations. Wherever possible, first on private property.

118136.explanation: We only consider implementation possible if there is agreement in the neighborhood between residents, entrepreneurs, and other directly involved parties.

118138.explanation: We want good and compassionate care that requires little adjustment to our daily lives, so you can stay living in your own neighborhood. However, given the staff shortage, it is not realistic to always consider personal preferences based on beliefs or religion.

118140.explanation: If people cannot afford these products because their income is too low, we must focus on strengthening employment and making it worthwhile. We do not solve income problems with free goods, but by giving people perspective on a better income.

118144.explanation: Entrepreneurs should be able to decide themselves when they want to be open, without unnecessary government restrictions. More shopping Sundays increase choice for residents and strengthen the local economy.

118145.explanation: Priority in allocation cannot be explained to other residents on the (long) waiting list. Status holders assigned to our municipality will initially be housed in (regional) transitional locations. There is no place in the Netherlands for rejected asylum seekers.

118147.explanation: We focus on a vital countryside with (agricultural) businesses that contribute to a green environment and attractive opportunities for recreation and nature experiences. This means no wind turbines and no solar fields. However, we do see opportunities for installation on flat roofs.

118149.explanation: We do not want brothels and/or coffeeshops in our municipality.

118150.explanation: We must focus on tackling inappropriate housing and building more middle-rent and owner-occupied homes, so people can move on and space frees up for those who truly need social rental housing.

118152.explanation: We continue to regulate and monitor the splitting of homes into smaller apartments and ‘verkamering’ to avoid increasing pressure on the neighborhood.

118154.explanation: Parking pressure is high in the centers and this hinders both residents and entrepreneurs. More parking spaces improve accessibility, livability, and an attractive (shopping) center. Therefore, we want to invest specifically in additional parking where the pressure is highest.

118158.explanation: For years there have been approved plans for the development of recreational homes in Vlietland. We support this development and construction of recreational homes and good integration in this part of the area.

118160.explanation: Leidschendam-Voorburg must not become a commuter town. Business activity and economic dynamism provide jobs and an attractive living and visiting environment. We therefore invest in our shopping centers.

118100.explanation: Leidschendam-Voorburg has an extensive participation regulation. This ensures the knowledge and skills of residents are involved in important municipal decisions.

118102.explanation: GBLV wants the lowest possible charges for residents and entrepreneurs.

118106.explanation: The municipality has agreements with the COA regarding the shelter of asylum seekers. These agreements are respected. However, the sheltering of asylum seekers must not delay existing construction plans. Therefore, sheltering in Overgoo should not last longer than agreed.

118110.explanation: The municipality must offer an alternative to the fireworks ban for private individuals. This does not necessarily have to be a fireworks show. GBLV prefers a more sustainable variant using, for example, drones and lasers.

118111.explanation: The municipality has just started charging fees for bulky waste collection. We await the results.

118115.explanation: There are now sufficient resources available for this.

118117.explanation: Culture is important. Organizations that provide music lessons should be eligible for subsidies.

118120.explanation: The municipality is growing and residents want visible enforcement officers on the streets.

118121.explanation: Yes, camera surveillance is an effective means to prevent nuisance or enforce rules, such as with parking violations.

118122.explanation: The women's shelters look for a safe place themselves. If they find this with us, they are of course welcome.

118125.explanation: Assign at least 50% to local residents.

118128.explanation: The economy is also important; this requires customized solutions.

118129.explanation: Young people and students prefer to live in the big city.

118130.explanation: Participating in society is important for a person's self-esteem. This can be through socially meaningful work, such as volunteering.

118134.explanation: There are sufficient charging points in the municipality.

118136.explanation: Parking permits are an effective means to prevent parking nuisance (for example around The Mall). Customized solutions around parking permits can be provided in consultation with neighborhood residents.

118140.explanation: Yes, for women and girls who cannot afford it.

118145.explanation: The houses need to be allocated sooner.

118150.explanation: We follow the provincial policy, which is set at 30%.

118152.explanation: Customized solutions are needed here due to, among other things, parking and the living environment.

118154.explanation: Mobility requires customized solutions; what is desired seems to vary per street, neighborhood, or shopping area.

118158.explanation: The costs involved are so high that it comes at the expense of many other important matters. We are not willing to pay for this. Buying off building rights is expensive.

118160.explanation: Revitalization is always good, but only in good consultation with the shopkeepers and local residents.

118100.explanation: The possibility to hold a referendum exists in our municipality. D66 wants to maintain this. D66 stands for various (innovative) forms of participation and has successfully made several proposals such as the children's question hour, a citizen's forum, and the right to challenge.

118101.explanation: D66 is in favor of citizen participation. We support residents who take the initiative to take over a municipal task and are able to do so.

118102.explanation: D66 wants municipal services to always be as accessible as possible. High costs run counter to this. In D66's view, municipal permit fees should be as cost-covering as possible.

118106.explanation: D66 Leidschendam-Voorburg stands for humane and just policies in the reception of asylum seekers. Therefore, we comply with the agreements arising from the distribution law.

118111.explanation: Existing policy; D66 wants the costs for waste disposal to be distributed as fairly as possible. Therefore, we believe that residents who want their bulky waste collected should pay a modest contribution.

118112.explanation: Attention must always be given to nuisance for residents during parties and festivals, but noise from events such as King's Day, A Walk Downtown, and the Stompwijk Horse Days characterizes the vibrant nature of Leidschendam-Voorburg.

118115.explanation: The local broadcaster in Leidschendam-Voorburg is an organization consisting almost entirely of volunteers. To support them and enable them to produce news for our residents as well as possible, D66 favors financial support for the local broadcaster.

118117.explanation: D66 Leidschendam-Voorburg believes every child should be able to participate: at school, at home, on the sports field, in nature, and in music. Education, sports, music, art, and culture strengthen each other, and we want to deepen that connection further.

118120.explanation: In recent years, we have invested in more enforcement officers (boa's) in our municipality. Therefore, we prefer to focus on good deployment of these officers and, where possible, stronger cooperation with the police.

118121.explanation: Camera surveillance can contribute to safety but is also an infringement on privacy. Therefore, D66 opts for cautious use: only in places where it is really necessary and not longer than strictly necessary.

118128.explanation: D66 is in favor of transforming office buildings into housing where possible. In this way, existing space can be used efficiently and local housing needs can be addressed.

118130.explanation: D66 wants to offer perspective to people who have been on social assistance for a long time. We do this by guiding them to paid work or, alternatively, through volunteer work as a step to build routine, self-confidence, and social contacts.

118132.explanation: The municipality of Leidschendam-Voorburg already has an above-average number of poverty schemes, in addition to existing national poverty schemes such as the energy allowance. D66 prefers to focus on the lagging reach and use of these schemes rather than raising the income limit in advance.

118136.explanation: In several neighborhoods in Leidschendam-Voorburg, there is high parking pressure due to 'foreign parkers.' Parking permits can be a good solution to reduce parking pressure and give residents of these neighborhoods priority for available parking spots.

118140.explanation: Distributing items for free is, in D66's view, a form of symptom treatment. Residents with a minimum income can make use of the poverty schemes provided by the municipality.

118144.explanation: Yes, entrepreneurs should, according to D66, be given as much space as possible to do business. Including on Sundays.

118145.explanation: D66 believes that status holders (refugees with a residence permit) should be assigned housing as quickly as possible. This enables them to participate in society as soon as possible, allowing them to learn the language, work, and build a social network.

118149.explanation: D66 sees no necessity in allowing a coffeeshop in Leidschendam-Voorburg.

118150.explanation: D66 prefers to focus on the turnover of residents in existing social rental housing who, based on their income, no longer belong there.

118152.explanation: D66 supports shared housing to address the housing needs of, for example, students and young people.

118158.explanation: D66 is not in favor of building in green areas, including in the Vlietland recreational area. The reality is that current zoning plans already allow developers to proceed with their building plans.

118160.explanation: Both shopping areas are outdated. The local function for daily groceries and local services and products underlines the local importance of these shopping areas. D66 supports investing in future-proof shopping areas that are pleasant to visit.

118100.explanation: The CDA is not in favor of a referendum. The municipal council is elected to make difficult decisions. However, the CDA believes that residents should be actively involved in decision-making.

118101.explanation: The CDA supports community initiatives by residents and the right to challenge.

118102.explanation: Fees should be as cost-covering as possible and should not be used as a steering instrument for the municipal budget.

118106.explanation: The CDA believes the municipality must comply with the distribution law. However, reception should always be accompanied by clear communication from the municipality and local support.

118110.explanation: The CDA supports the fireworks ban and wants to organize an alternative to consumer fireworks so that residents can celebrate the New Year together in a pleasant way.

118111.explanation: Waste processing costs money. We pay waste charges for this. The CDA wants to keep these charges as low as possible. By collecting bulky waste for a fee, the waste charges can remain as low as possible. The user pays.

118112.explanation: Hearing damage has a lasting impact on someone's life and costs the community money for care. Therefore, excessive noise nuisance must be prevented, and when granting permits for festivals or events, the municipality must make a careful consideration of the noise impact on visitors and the surrounding area.

118115.explanation: Broadcasters receive money from the municipality via the national government. A national proposal is forthcoming for additional funding to local broadcasters from the national government.

118117.explanation: The CDA wants sports and culture to be accessible to all children. Therefore, the CDA supports children in low-income families receiving discounts for sports and culture via the Ooievaarspas. However, the CDA believes the municipality should not subsidize children whose parents can afford music lessons.

118120.explanation: The CDA wants to increase the visibility of enforcement in the municipality. The CDA wants to shape this together with enforcers. This may require more enforcers.

118121.explanation: The CDA wants to increase safety in our municipality. Extra camera surveillance can detect crime more effectively and quickly, and greatly helps the police in tackling crime.

118122.explanation: The CDA believes more must be done to prevent violence against women. A women's shelter can help with this. If it is possible to realize, the CDA supports it.

118125.explanation: We want priority for people with local ties, so that young people can continue living in their own village or city and older people can move within their own environment or municipality.

118128.explanation: The CDA believes vacant offices should be converted into housing. The CDA thinks this can be done without subsidies. However, the municipality should actively seek cooperation with project developers or housing corporations to achieve this.

118129.explanation: The CDA supports housing for young people. Given the current housing shortage, the CDA believes that seemingly less usual forms of housing may be considered. The development of the youth must remain the priority.

118130.explanation: The CDA finds it important that people who need help from society receive it. However, the CDA believes residents should then give something back to society in an appropriate way. This could mean looking for a job or doing volunteer work.

118132.explanation: Local poverty policy is a supplement to national poverty policy. The national government can give municipalities more money to apply a higher income threshold. The CDA is therefore critical of the current situation in which larger municipalities with more funds can apply higher thresholds.

118134.explanation: The CDA considers it important to have sufficient charging points for electric cars. These should be placed based on demand, where people with electric cars live and work. Residents should be able to submit an application to the municipality for this.

118136.explanation: Free parking remains the standard as far as the CDA is concerned. The CDA chooses a tailored approach per neighborhood and wants to introduce (affordable) permit parking only where necessary.

118138.explanation: The CDA believes choice in healthcare is important. However, the municipality cannot always facilitate and finance that people can always choose a healthcare provider that matches their faith, culture, or belief.

118140.explanation: The CDA finds period poverty an important problem and ensured in the past council period that the municipality distributed free tampons.

118144.explanation: A fixed day of rest each week is good for health and community connection. The CDA does not want more shops to open on Sundays than is currently the case.

118145.explanation: Every municipality is obligated to accommodate a number of status holders. The municipality must meet this obligation and ensure sufficient housing with housing corporations. Status holders are subject to the same priority rules as residents.

118147.explanation: The CDA believes solar energy should contribute to achieving our climate goals. Therefore, the CDA does not want to prohibit solar panels on agricultural land upfront.

118149.explanation: The CDA is against the establishment of coffeeshops.

118150.explanation: The CDA wants 30% of homes to be in the social segment and another 30% in the mid-price segment. 40% social housing would make too many projects unfeasible and would actually reduce the number of social homes.

118152.explanation: Part of society needs room rentals, for example students or young people. There should be space for this. However, renting by the room must not put pressure on neighborhood livability.

118154.explanation: There are sufficient parking spaces for local residents in Leidschendam, Voorburg, and Stompwijk. However, parking for visitors, especially at the Mall of the Netherlands, needs to be examined.

118158.explanation: The CDA supports preserving a green Vlietland, but the developer may build there. This has already been decided. Blocking this would cost the municipality a lot of money. Preserving a green Vlietland can only be realized through joint consultation between the municipality, province, and developer.

118160.explanation: The CDA believes the Damlaan and Julianabaan should be vibrant shopping areas. The municipality can invest in this. However, the CDA voted against the current renovation plans for the Julianabaan due to concerns about traffic flow. We must prevent a second 'Mall'.

118100.explanation: Thanks to the SP, the municipality of Leidschendam-Voorburg has the possibility of a referendum. Residents can take the initiative to hold a referendum.

118101.explanation: The management of these facilities must be well arranged. Feedback and comments from residents must be listened to.

118102.explanation: Permit prices must be reasonable. The unnecessary requirement to have permits, such as for legalization, must stop.

118106.explanation: The reception of asylum seekers is a statutory task mandated from higher authorities. The municipality of Leidschendam-Voorburg does not need to take a leading role in asylum seeker reception.

118110.explanation: A fireworks show or an old-year’s event like in the past is very desirable. It may help reduce vandalism.

118111.explanation: For people with little money, it is very important that bulky waste is collected for free. In general, free bulky waste collection prevents illegal dumping and overcrowding.

118112.explanation: There are regulations for noise standards at performances. These are sufficient and may remain as they are.

118115.explanation: The local broadcaster is important. Financial support is crucial to prevent the local broadcaster from falling into the hands of commercial parties.

118117.explanation: Music lessons are part of a good development for children. Children of parents with less money should also be able to take music lessons.

118120.explanation: The municipality has sufficient enforcers. If there are real shortages, expansion can be considered.

118121.explanation: We only allow camera surveillance in exceptional situations. Privacy remains important.

118122.explanation: Violence against women is a serious problem. Several women's shelters must be established in the region, including in Leidschendam-Voorburg.

118125.explanation: Giving priority to people from the municipality increases social cohesion.

118128.explanation: Even better is to take ownership to make the homes cheaper (without profit).

118129.explanation: The SP supports a large project with container homes or tiny houses to address the housing shortage. A meadow area can be made suitable by installing water and sewage systems. Otherwise, the homes can be self-sufficient.

118130.explanation: The SP is against 'working without pay'. People who are able to work should be helped with real jobs with a fair wage. Volunteer work can only be done if it is truly voluntary.

118132.explanation: The income limit to receive assistance should be at least equal to the level applied in the municipality of The Hague.

118134.explanation: There must be sufficient parking spaces for cars that are not electric. Car owners remain responsible for charging their electric vehicles themselves.

118136.explanation: Permits cost residents money. The cause of parking nuisance must provide solutions. When permits are necessary, the party causing the parking problems pays for them.

118138.explanation: There must be adequate opportunities for nearby care. Reinvestment is needed in more spots for general practitioners and other healthcare providers.

118140.explanation: Girls and women with low incomes must be able to use good hygiene products. The free products should be easily accessible and respect privacy.

118144.explanation: There are shopping Sundays in our municipality. The problem with shopping Sundays is that small entrepreneurs have fewer opportunities than large companies.

118145.explanation: There must be sufficient social rental housing so that priority is not needed.

118147.explanation: Banning goes too far. Solar panels must be properly integrated to limit horizon pollution.

118149.explanation: There should be room for a small number of coffeeshops in the municipality. The choice of location is important.

118150.explanation: The percentage of social rental housing must increase. Our municipality lags behind the Netherlands and the need is great.

118152.explanation: The SP is against slumlords. There should be real homes so people can build a life.

118154.explanation: Parking spaces must be well integrated. A single residential project without fixed parking spaces may be a good initiative and meets a need.

118158.explanation: The plans no longer fit the current times. More value is placed on nature.

118160.explanation: Modernizing the Julianabaan with good parking facilities is desirable. The Dam can be made much more attractive by making it low-traffic or car-free (time slots).

118100.explanation: The ChristenUnie finds it important to involve residents openly and honestly early on in policy choices and/or major developments. We should therefore prevent a last resort like a referendum.

118101.explanation: The ChristenUnie finds it important that residents who organize themselves are also given the opportunity to take over a municipal task.

118102.explanation: Municipal permits are currently as cost-covering as possible; the ChristenUnie supports this.

118106.explanation: The ChristenUnie advocates following the Distribution Law, so we must actually do more. The ChristenUnie supports small-scale reception, spread throughout the entire municipality.

118110.explanation: The ChristenUnie supports facilitating local year-end clubs that organize their own neighborhood party, from a small-scale fireworks show (under conditions) to an innovative drone show.

118111.explanation: The ChristenUnie sees waste as a resource. It is good that residents therefore pay a small contribution for the collection of bulky waste.

118112.explanation: It is good if we consider each other; this means both clarity about how long extra noise is allowed and also that there are exceptions for a festival or party.

118115.explanation: Democracy is under pressure, which requires journalism that can critically question politics. It is worthwhile to invest in this.

118117.explanation: The ChristenUnie finds cultural education important for all children. Children from families in poverty can receive discounts or participate for free via the Ooievaarspas.

118120.explanation: In the past four years, there has been extra investment in 10 additional enforcement officers. This will not need to be done again in the near future.

118121.explanation: Camera surveillance can have a preventive effect, but the ChristenUnie wants to apply it limitedly (proportionate) and embed it in proper legislation (privacy).

118122.explanation: We help people trapped in relationship violence, human trafficking, or prostitution to get out. A women's shelter helps with this.

118125.explanation: Many people want to move within their own neighborhood to a house that fits their life phase; the ChristenUnie wants to focus on this. At the same time, there is a need for homeless families, to whom we want to give priority for housing.

118128.explanation: We do everything possible to solve the housing shortage. Transforming vacant offices is a great opportunity. It is not necessary to allocate subsidies for this.

118129.explanation: We want to focus our energy on real homes and building communities where people can be there for each other and where everyone feels recognized.

118130.explanation: The ChristenUnie finds it important to emphasize that people are more than just their earning capacity. Volunteer work or participating in society in other ways can be very valuable, especially for someone's well-being.

118132.explanation: Currently, people earning up to 110% of the statutory minimum wage can make use of the minimum policy options. The ChristenUnie finds this too low, especially when in surrounding municipalities it is around 130-150%.

118134.explanation: The number of charging stations will be expanded to accommodate the growth of electric cars. We advocate for smart charging stations so that electric cars can serve as a buffer in the electricity network.

118136.explanation: Neighborhoods bordering The Hague often suffer from parkers from The Hague. We will therefore have to seriously consider paid parking or expanding parking permits in these neighborhoods for livability.

118138.explanation: This is not a municipal choice.

118140.explanation: The minimum policy must be increased to support people on benefits and workers with large and small expenses.

118144.explanation: The ChristenUnie is against expanding Sunday opening hours for shops. Furthermore, shopkeepers should not be forced to open their shops on Sundays. The municipality must guarantee and monitor this.

118145.explanation: The ChristenUnie finds it important that our municipality participates in the reception of refugees and status holders.

118147.explanation: The ChristenUnie wants to prioritize solar panels on roofs first and therefore expects that solar panels on agricultural land are not necessary in the energy transition in Leidschendam-Voorburg. Small-scale initiatives by farmers to contribute themselves are welcomed.

118149.explanation: The ChristenUnie is against coffeeshops, casinos, and gambling halls in Leidschendam-Voorburg. Alcohol, drugs, and gambling cause significant damage in people's lives. The ChristenUnie wants to combat addiction and drug-related problems.

118150.explanation: More affordable housing is needed in Leidschendam-Voorburg. Over the next ten years, we aim for 30% social housing at the municipal level. Therefore, in the coming years, we will temporarily impose 40% social housing per project to reach this share.

118152.explanation: We want to prevent overcrowding and ensure safe homes. That is why we focus on supervision of proper renting of homes through the Good Landlord Act.

118154.explanation: For new large housing projects, it is always considered whether shared mobility can be part of the project. This way, new residents immediately know how to travel to and from their new home. This is taken into account in the parking standards.

118158.explanation: We want to prevent the Vlietlanden nature reserve from being built over with holiday homes. Therefore, the ChristenUnie has taken the initiative for an alternative plan to build a holiday park in Stompwijk instead. This creates opportunities for nature development and the construction of affordable homes.

118160.explanation: The Damcentrum deserves additional attention as a local shopping center, according to the ChristenUnie, just as attention was given recently to the Julianabaan and the Huygenskwartier.

118100.explanation: Many residents want to have a say in their neighborhood or district, and their involvement is becoming increasingly important. The Party for the Animals (PvdD) wants the municipality to carefully weigh residents' initiatives, plans, or concerns and to include advisory referendums for important decisions.

118101.explanation: The PvdD advocates for good sports facilities and wants more space for nature and biodiversity. It is important for people to get to know and appreciate nature. This is strengthened by involving residents and providing good guidance from the municipality.

118102.explanation: Municipal permits must be adequately covered in the budget. The PvdD will assess proposals on a case-by-case basis, taking into account the impact on sustainability and consideration for those with tight finances.

118106.explanation: Leidschendam-Voorburg consistently takes a fair share in providing shelter places for refugees.

118110.explanation: The PvdD wants to transform the New Year's tradition in the interest of animals, nature, the environment, and our safety into professional fireworks shows. Festive, safe, and environmentally friendly alternatives such as light shows are preferable.

118111.explanation: For municipal levies, including waste charges, residents who produce less waste pay lower fees. Collection or disposal of bulky waste is free up to a certain amount.

118112.explanation: Events must be festive, safe, and environmentally friendly. They can take place if people, animals, nature, and the environment do not suffer damage. Preventing noise nuisance to the surroundings and avoiding hearing damage to visitors is important.

118115.explanation: Strong, independent local media and regional broadcasters are very important for the residents of Leidschendam-Voorburg. There will be no budget cuts for this. To promote independent (investigative) journalism, a widely accessible local media fund will be established for non-commercial local media.

118117.explanation: The PvdD wants the municipality to invest in sports, swimming lessons, culture, music lessons, and (independent) nature and sustainability education in schools. The municipality also encourages lessons where children learn to repair products.

118120.explanation: Deploying municipal enforcement officers is useful to reduce nuisance and increase safety. The municipality must always make a wise choice to organize deployment as effectively as possible to guarantee the safety of animals, people, and nature.

118121.explanation: CCTV in public spaces is used very sparingly due to privacy considerations. CCTV provides only illusory security and often only causes displacement of the problem.

118122.explanation: The PvdD finds it important that people fleeing domestic violence are well accommodated. A broad approach for the entire chain of prevention, shelter, therapy, and criminal justice must take place regionally. Whether shelter in our municipality is needed is not yet clear.

118125.explanation: That people currently sometimes have to wait fifteen years for social rental housing is unacceptable. By creating more living space and promoting flow, we shorten the waiting lists. Young people can stay in their birthplace and elderly can live close to their loved ones.

118128.explanation: The PvdD wants to use existing buildings more efficiently, for example by transforming office buildings. The municipality can assist owners in this, but the costs remain with the owners.

118129.explanation: The shortage of student housing will be solved by quickly realizing sustainable homes, preferably in vacant (office) buildings. Where there is a severe shortage, flexible housing can be used, provided they are sustainable and fully reusable. The municipality does not bear the costs.

118130.explanation: We no longer require people entitled to social assistance to perform a counter-performance.

118132.explanation: Leidschendam-Voorburg expands the minimum income policy to incomes up to 130% of the social minimum.

118134.explanation: The municipality provides sufficient charging points, more than currently available, with green electricity for electric vehicles and vessels. These are integrated into the existing streetscape where possible, for example in the ground or in lampposts.

118136.explanation: The PvdD wants paid parking in the urban area throughout the municipality and wants to ensure in the longer term that residents can park their cars in nearby garages at a lower rate, with access to free bicycle parking and inexpensive public transport connections.

118138.explanation: Care providers and shelter locations take into account the background, identity, and lifestyle of people needing care. Conversely, care providers must be able to practice their profession without their background, identity, and lifestyle being questioned.

118140.explanation: The PvdD wants more effort to alleviate and prevent poverty. The inability to afford menstrual products causes stress and increases health risks. People experiencing menstrual poverty sometimes have to call in sick at work or school, leading to unequal opportunities.

118144.explanation: Stores in places like the Mall are already open on all Sundays of the year. Allowing more shopping Sundays is therefore impossible.

118145.explanation: The municipality meets the target for housing status holders and opposes the proposed law that removes priority for status holders for social rental housing. If the law is unexpectedly passed, Leidschendam-Voorburg will create a local priority scheme for status holders.

118147.explanation: Agricultural land is not intended for solar fields but for plant food production and nature development and, if livestock farming shrinks significantly, for housing construction. Many (business) buildings in the municipality are still suitable for solar panel installation.

118149.explanation: The sale of soft drugs is best done through legal channels. Establishing sales near schools is unwise. In addition, education about the risks of soft drug use is important.

118150.explanation: The PvdD wants more affordable homes and therefore wants at least 40% of new homes to be social rental housing.

118152.explanation: The PvdD sometimes sees rooming houses and/or splitting homes as a solution to the shortage of suitable housing. At the neighborhood level, it must be assessed whether this fits well. It must not come at the expense of public space. Splitting must comply with clear rules to prevent abuses.

118154.explanation: No new parking spaces for cars will be added on the streets. The PvdD prioritizes the best possible public transport and improving cycling facilities.

118158.explanation: The PvdD wants no building in Vlietland and that the municipality makes maximum efforts to prevent the plan to build holiday homes there. Vlietland remains a nature reserve.

118160.explanation: The shopping areas Damlaan and Julianabaan are not sustainable. The PvdD supports investments in more public green areas, improving cycling facilities, and measures to restrict car traffic.

118100.explanation: We want to strengthen democracy with structural citizen participation and do not opt for a referendum. Referendums simplify complex choices and often yield unclear mandates. If little is done with the outcome, trust and involvement deteriorate.

118101.explanation: Management of a sports hall or park by residents can have many positive and connecting effects. Those involved must receive the right (financial) support; otherwise, it won't work long-term. Agreements must be clear and straightforward, without bureaucracy.

118102.explanation: Fees must cover costs and should not be a source of profit.

118106.explanation: Distribution and solidarity are necessary. We support small-scale, humane shelter locations spread throughout the municipality, with neighborhood involvement from the start. This promotes integration, ensures agreed commitments are kept, and ensures shelter benefits the neighborhood.

118110.explanation: We support the municipality helping to shape 'the new New Year's Eve' by supporting initiatives from residents and possibly organizing a program for each village center. This can include fireworks, but there are also fun programs to be devised that cause less inconvenience for people, animals, and the environment.

118111.explanation: Bulky waste should be collected in an accessible way. A new system has just been introduced where the user pays part of the costs themselves. The current arrangement seems to lead to more illegal dumping in some neighborhoods. We would not prefer to change back immediately: stable policy is important.

118112.explanation: In any case, enforcement of the standards must be consistent and stricter standards may also be necessary. There is noise pollution, and the livability in the centers of Leidschendam and Voorburg must be safeguarded.

118115.explanation: Independent local journalism strengthens democracy and transparency. Therefore, we believe it is desirable that the municipality supports the local broadcaster and establishes a media fund for independent, local journalistic research.

118117.explanation: We agree. Cultural education increases opportunities and personal development. Subsidized music lessons must be accessible to all children and offered by qualified teachers and organizations, to ensure quality and prevent segregation.

118120.explanation: We disagree. Leidschendam-Voorburg is a relatively safe municipality. Therefore, more enforcement officers are not the solution. We opt for smart, neighborhood-focused enforcement supplemented with neighborhood watch, neighborhood apps, and cooperation with residents, which strengthens both safety and social cohesion.

118121.explanation: Privacy and prevention deserve priority over surveillance. Camera surveillance is only justified when it can be demonstrated to effectively contribute to safety and other measures are insufficient. Camera surveillance should never be a standard solution, but remains a last resort.

118122.explanation: Safety for women requires concrete provisions. Women's shelters must be accessible and easy to reach. Where safely possible, it should be ensured that children can continue attending school and activities.

118125.explanation: Connection with one's own municipality is relevant, and solidarity and regional agreements are also important. We advocate for a points system that grants extra priority to certain groups. Being a resident of this municipality should be one of these criteria.

118128.explanation: As far as this is still possible, we should definitely do so. It is smart to make use of available space and yields more homes faster.

118129.explanation: We agree. The focus is mainly on structural solutions and affordability. However, temporary student housing can also help, especially considering the aging population. Temporary arrangements for student cohabitation can also contribute to better use of existing housing.

118130.explanation: Participation works better when based on trust and intrinsic motivation than obligation. Moreover, not everyone is capable of doing volunteer work.

118132.explanation: This is urgently needed and prevents people from falling through the cracks.

118134.explanation: As more electric cars appear, the supply of charging facilities must grow accordingly.

118136.explanation: We agree. Space in our municipality is limited and we want more room for pedestrians, cyclists, and greenery. Unlimited and almost free parking no longer fits this. Moreover, there is parking pressure from surrounding areas. We choose livability.

118138.explanation: Human scale and appropriate care are important. A good healthcare professional aligns with the needs of the client or patient. If there is no good match, it must be possible to choose another professional to keep care appropriate.

118140.explanation: We agree with combating period poverty, but preferably not through isolated measures. We choose a broad welfare approach that structurally addresses poverty. Where menstrual products are offered, we prefer sustainable alternatives, such as reusable products.

118144.explanation: Mandatory Sunday closure is no longer realistic in Leidschendam-Voorburg. With the Mall and supermarkets already open, this is outdated. We opt for freedom for entrepreneurs, with attention to staff and neighbors.

118145.explanation: In the event of housing shortages, a fair distribution is necessary. Refugees with residence permits do not deserve automatic priority but should be carefully included in a transparent points system where various urgencies are weighed.

118147.explanation: Priority lies with solar panels on roofs and along infrastructure.

118149.explanation: Given the scale and livability of Leidschendam-Voorburg and the existing supply in the region, we do not want to permit new coffeeshops in our municipality.

118150.explanation: There is a great shortage of social rental housing, demanding strong public oversight. We want new construction to genuinely contribute to social housing and therefore do not allow developers to buy off their obligations. Social homes must actually be built.

118152.explanation: We agree. Renting out individual rooms should be possible, as shared living can help address the housing shortage. This can only be done with clear rules to prevent nuisance. Splitting homes into independent mini-apartments is different and we want to limit that.

118154.explanation: We disagree. Space in the village centers is scarce and we prefer to use it for greenery, pedestrians, and cyclists. Instead of more parking spaces, we opt for better cycling facilities and a more pleasant public space.

118158.explanation: Nature and recreation for residents must have priority. The value of Vlietland extends beyond the municipal border and requires protection at the regional level. We do this together with involved residents and social organizations, so the area remains green, accessible, and for everyone.

118160.explanation: Lively centers are important for meeting and economy. This requires more than money: listening to residents and entrepreneurs. Local entrepreneurs demonstrably invest more in their neighborhood and social cohesion. Therefore, we choose fewer chains and more local retailers, makers, and cultural activities.

118100.explanation: Attention-LV wants every petition or residents' initiative with at least 250 signatures from the municipality to be discussed in the municipal council. We owe it to our residents to listen to their wishes.

118101.explanation: Society is you! If residents want to contribute to their living environment themselves, this should be possible in good consultation.

118102.explanation: Prices are already high enough. However, the municipality should respond sooner regarding the granting or refusal of permits. It currently takes too long, causing residents to wait too long for the green light.

118106.explanation: If there is nuisance, intervention must occur, if necessary by closure. Of course, nuisance must be prevented. Shelter in our municipality must be humane. People deserve dignified shelter. Humane shelter leads to less nuisance for the surroundings. We all benefit from that.

118110.explanation: Even better would be if the municipality organizes a drone show or another collective New Year's celebration. Also, neighborhoods should have the opportunity to organize a neighborhood New Year's Eve party that fits their community.

118111.explanation: Our municipality has no recycling center. Those who want to dispose of bulky waste themselves must go to Rijswijk. Not everyone has this opportunity. To prevent illegal dumping, bulky waste is therefore collected free of charge.

118112.explanation: There are legal noise standards. We do not have many parties and festivals in our municipality. For the few times per year that we gather festively, noise standards do not need to be relaxed—unless there are nuisance reports from neighbors.

118115.explanation: Residents should be informed about what happens in our municipality through the local broadcaster. National and regional media have a different scope. Therefore, it is important that we support our local broadcaster.

118125.explanation: People who grew up in this municipality, and people who perform socially essential professions here (teachers, healthcare workers, police, firefighters) receive priority.

118129.explanation: Not only for students but also for working, non-studying young people (18-28 years) who want to live independently.

118134.explanation: Not everyone drives an electric car or can afford one.

118149.explanation: No coffeeshop nuisance in our municipality. The Hague is close by and has sufficient coffeeshops.

118150.explanation: More space is needed in the middle segment (purchase and rent). Many people earn just too much to qualify for social housing and too little for home ownership. Building for the middle segment should receive more space and be encouraged.

118160.explanation: The Julianabaan needs renovation, but the large-scale €21 million renovation is too extensive and too costly where only an upgrade is needed. Investment should be made to attract more visitors to Damlaan and around the Sluis.

statements.title: The municipality should invest more money in the shopping areas De Damlaan and Julianabaan.
Noise standards at festivals and parties must be made stricter.
Residents of Leidschendam-Voorburg should be able to express their opinion on important issues through a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Leidschendam-Voorburg moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The asylum reception center in our municipality should be closed as soon as possible.

titleParts.glossary: A place where people who have applied for asylum are accommodated. An asylum shelter can be temporary. A permanent asylum shelter is called an asylum seekers' centre (azc).

titleParts.text: in onze gemeente moet zo snel mogelijk sluiten.

statements.title: The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: Residents should be able to take over the management of, for example, a sports hall or a public green space from the municipality.
Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: The prices of municipal permits must be raised.

titleParts.glossary: Permission from the municipality to do something.

statements.title: At least 40% of newly built homes in Leidschendam-Voorburg should be social housing. Currently 28% of homes in the Netherlands are social housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Leidschendam-Voorburg moet minimaal 40% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is nu 28% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: Leidschendam-Voorburg should establish a shelter for victims of domestic violence.

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: Leidschendam-Voorburg should introduce a parking-permit system in more neighborhoods.

titleParts.text: Leidschendam-Voorburg moet in meer wijken een

titleParts.glossary: A permit to park in a certain place. You have to pay for that right.

statements.title: The municipality should allow more Sunday shopping days.
Vacant offices should be converted into housing with subsidies from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente worden omgebouwd tot woningen.

statements.title: Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
There should be more parking spaces in the town centers.

titleParts.text: Er moeten meer parkeerplaatsen komen in de

titleParts.glossary: Villages or small towns that are part of the municipality.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
The municipality should allow landlords to rent out individual rooms instead of entire houses.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
The municipality should ban solar panels on agricultural land.
People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
Leidschendam-Voorburg should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) focused on public safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The municipality should give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: Leidschendam-Voorburg should install more CCTV/camera surveillance.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality should distribute tampons and sanitary pads for free.
When allocating social housing, applicants from the municipality should be given priority.

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: moeten woningzoekenden uit de eigen gemeente voorrang krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality should allow the establishment of new coffeeshops.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Shops or cafés where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: The municipality must invest in temporary housing for students, for example container housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld steken in tijdelijke huisvesting voor studenten, bijvoorbeeld

titleParts.glossary: Moveable and temporary homes, usually made from a shipping container.

statements.title: The municipality must stop the new holiday park in the Vlietland recreation area.
